context: Tests whether the agent performs a Rails security review following the rails-security-review skill: covering the correct review areas in order, classifying findings by correct severity levels, and producing output with all four required fields per finding.

checklist.name: Auth/authz reviewed first
checklist.description: The review report addresses authentication or authorization issues before or separately from parameter, query, or output issues — the first finding or section covers auth concerns

checklist.name: Parameter handling reviewed
checklist.description: The review explicitly addresses how parameters are handled (strong params, mass assignment, or permit patterns)

checklist.name: Query safety reviewed
checklist.description: The review addresses SQL query construction, injection risk, or use of parameterized queries

checklist.name: High severity: SQL injection identified
checklist.description: The SQL injection vulnerability in the provided code is classified as High severity (not Medium or Low)

checklist.name: High severity: missing authz identified
checklist.description: The missing authorization check is classified as High severity

checklist.name: Medium severity finding identified
checklist.description: At least one finding is correctly classified as Medium severity (e.g. sensitive data logging, unscoped mass assignment, or weak filtering)

checklist.name: Attack path per finding
checklist.description: Each High severity finding includes an attack path — a description of how an attacker would exploit the issue

checklist.name: Affected file per finding
checklist.description: Each finding names the specific file (and ideally line or method) where the vulnerability exists

checklist.name: Mitigation per finding
checklist.description: Each finding includes a smallest credible mitigation — a concrete, actionable fix rather than a generic recommendation

checklist.name: Exploitability focus
checklist.description: The review prioritizes findings that are directly exploitable (e.g. no auth check, raw string interpolation in query) over style issues — does NOT lead with stylistic concerns

checklist.name: Secrets and output reviewed
checklist.description: The review addresses at least one of: secrets in code/logs, unsafe redirects, or output rendering (HTML/XSS) concerns
